title: SAP Fiori Screen Spec
description: Contract between Claude's reasoning output and the Figma plugin's construction input. Claude generates this; the plugin consumes it. Both sides validate against this schema.

$schema.description: Must reference this schema version. Allows the plugin to reject incompatible specs.

meta.description: Reasoning provenance — explains WHY this design was chosen. Used for auditing, testing, and rationale docs.

requirement.description: The original requirement or ticket text that was analyzed.

floorplan.description: The SAP Fiori floorplan selected for this screen.

floorplanRationale.description: One sentence explaining why this floorplan was chosen over alternatives.

rationale.description: Summary of key design decisions. Referenced in the Step 7 rationale document.

validationStatus.description: Registry validation result. Must be 'pass' before the plugin runs.

unverifiedComponents.description: Components that failed registry lookup. Non-empty means validationStatus must be 'fail'.

screen.description: Top-level screen configuration. Logical only — no pixel values.

name.description: Human-readable screen name, used as the Figma frame label.

density.description: SAP density mode. Cozy for general/touch, Compact for power-user desktop.

title.description: Browser/app title for the screen. Used in ShellBar productName if not set on the component.

hierarchy.description: The component tree. Top-level items are direct children of the screen frame (typically ShellBar + content page).

Node.description: A single component or layout node in the hierarchy.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier within this spec. Used for referencing in tests and rationale docs.

component.description: SAP component identifier. Must match an entry in the verified registry (knowledge/schemas/). Canonical form is 'sap.m.Button' or short form 'Button' — plugin resolves both.

intent.description: One of the intentTags from the component's registry entry. Tells the plugin which variant to select when the component has multiple purposes.

props.description: Non-default property values only. The plugin applies SAP library defaults for everything not listed here. Keys must match property names in the component's registry schema.

kitProps.description: PREFERRED over props when present. Exact SAP Web UI Kit component property names and values as read from the live Figma library (e.g. {"Form Factor":"Compact","Interaction State":"Regular","Value State":"None"}). Claude resolves these via search_design_system + get_context_for_code_connect before generating the spec. Plugin calls instance.setProperties(kitProps) directly — no alias table needed.

kitVariableKeys.description: Pre-resolved SAP Horizon variable keys from the SAP Web UI Kit for fills/strokes/spacing on THIS node. Claude looks up the exact variable key via search_design_system. Plugin calls importVariableByKeyAsync(key) then setBoundVariable() directly — no whitelist lookup needed. Keys: fill, stroke, textFill, itemSpacing, paddingTop, paddingRight, paddingBottom, paddingLeft, cornerRadius.

kitIconKey.description: Pre-resolved SAP Web UI Kit icon component key (40 chars) for the icon on this node. Claude looks up the exact key from ICON_KEYS or via importComponentByKeyAsync. Plugin calls importComponentByKeyAsync(kitIconKey) then swaps the INSTANCE_SWAP property — no ICON_KEYS lookup needed.

label.description: Display label — used as button text, column header, panel title, etc. Shorthand for the most common text property so it doesn't need to live in props.

children.description: Child nodes. Must satisfy the parent component's allowed aggregations per the registry composition rules.

slots.description: Named slot assignments (for components with multiple named aggregations like DynamicPage which has title, header, content).

repeat.description: Render N copies of this node with sample data. Used for table rows, list items. Avoids enumerating identical nodes.

sampleData.description: Key-value pairs for sample content. Plugin substitutes these into repeated nodes or into text props. Keys match prop names.

oneOf.description: Array of values cycled across repeated nodes

state.description: Interaction or validation state for this node. Plugin selects the corresponding Figma variant.

note.description: Designer annotation. Not rendered by the plugin — appears as a Figma component annotation if supported, otherwise ignored.

meta.requirement: As a procurement manager, I need to review and approve pending purchase orders daily.
meta.floorplanRationale: Pre-curated queue for a known daily routine, not open-ended search — Worklist not List Report.
meta.rationale: Worklist with Responsive Table. ShellBar for navigation shell. IllustratedMessage for empty state. ObjectStatus for approval state visualization.
